items.title: Indians forced to return home as Iran war rocks Gulf economy
items.description: The Iran war has forced thousands of Indian workers to leave their jobs in Gulf nations and return home. Their future remains unclear, and now India itself is feeling the economic impact.

items.title: French foreign minister Barrot voices concern about 'terrorist threat' in the Sahel
items.description: France's foreign minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Friday voiced concern about the spread of Islamist extremism in west Africa, after talks with Togo's leader, Faure Gnassingbe. Violence by jihadist groups has blighted the Sahel region for the last decade, and is now spreading into northern regions of coastal countries such as Togo.

items.title: Petrol stations fined for mismatched pricing, misleading consumers
items.description: NSW Fair Trading issues more than 200 fines to petrol stations found to have breached pricing rules. Meanwhile, motorists celebrate a drop in prices as the premier weighs future fuel security measures.

items.title: Pro-Palestinian activists break into suspected Elbit Systems drone factory
items.description: Pro-Palestine activists have broken into a UK factory where they said drones were being made for Israel’s military.

items.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
items.description: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

items.title: Signal praises Apple over security fix in new iPhone update
items.description: The encrypted messaging app says the update fixes an issue that allowed the FBI to message notification content.

items.title: Intel soars on signs AI boom for CPUs is here
EU leaders discuss Iran crisis in Cyprus
items.description: European leaders meeting in Cyprus have pledged to step up efforts to help resolve conflicts in the Middle East, as the United States continues to criticize Europe for what it sees as insufficient support in countering Iran, with the EU largely remaining on the sidelines, while French President Emmanuel Macron stressed that restoring stability quickly is in everyone’s interest and key to reassuring global economies, ahead of talks with regional partners from Egypt, Lebanon and Syria and remarks from EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas. France24 journalist Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

items.title: Milei’s ruling party sees Senate as best chance of success for controversial bills
items.description: Milei government is giving priority to the Senate thanks to La Libertad Avanza caucus chief Patricia Bullrich well-assembled majority; Controversial reforms include changes to disability benefits and mental health rules, private property, evictions and the scrapping of limits on foreign ownership of land in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Ruthless Demons notch big win as Richmond's winless season continues
items.description: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

items.title: Samsung's chip output drops overnight as workers protest over pay, union says
Uzbekistan to sell 30% stake in National Investment Fund in London, Tashkent IPOs
Woodside tells inquiry no projects 'would survive' with additional tax
items.description: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

items.title: 20,000 teddy bears in Washington highlight abducted Ukrainian children
items.description: A sea of 20,000 teddy bears on the US National Mall highlights Ukrainian children abducted since 2021, urging action as the war’s toll on families grows.

items.title: 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster
items.description: This Sunday marks 40 years since the Chernobyl disaster, which the UN says affected more than 3.5 million people and contaminated nearly 50,000 square kilometres of land, and which remains the worst nuclear accident in history, as France Télévisions gains rare access to the site for a closer look.

items.title: Chornobyl at 40: Settlers and horses survive Russian drones, contamination
items.description: A radiation survivor and expert, an elderly returnee, and a wildlife researcher paint a picture of life 40 years later.

items.title: US Defense Secretary Hegseth: 'US blockade has gone global'
items.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Operation Epic Fury has achieved a decisive result in just weeks, focused on preventing Iran from developing nuclear weapons. He noted the mission is entering a new phase, giving Iran the chance to pursue a “good and wise” peace deal, while emphasising the strength of the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, soon to be reinforced by a second aircraft carrier. France24 journalist Shirli Sitbon shares further details on the press conference.

items.title: Women speak of violence during childbirth in DR Congo
items.description: Arecent viral video showing a young woman being hit by a doctor shortly after giving birth in a Kinshasa hospital has shocked the nation.

items.title: Pakistan military kills 22 in northwest clashes, child dead in crossfire
items.description: "A joint military operation was conducted by security forces and law enforcement agencies" that resulted in "intense exchange of fire" in which the militants were killed, Says military media wing statement

items.title: Former high-profile NT doctor to stand trial on rape, gross indecency charges
items.description: A former high-profile Northern Territory doctor charged with multiple sexual offences, including rape, has been committed to stand trial later this year.

items.title: Burkinabé architect Francis Kere builds sustainable cultural centre in Senegal
items.description: Francis Kere, a citizen of both Burkina Faso and Germany, is the only African to have won the prestigious Pritzker Prize for architecture.

items.title: Cosmo tanker could reach Japan on Sunday with first US shipment post-Iran war
Iranians bring music to sites devastated by US-Israeli attacks
items.description: Iranian musicians perform on city streets and venture to sites devastated in US-Israeli attacks.

items.title: Nigeria sets up court martial over alleged 2025 coup plot
items.description: Nigeria has inaugurated a military court to try officers accused of involvement in a failed coup attempt, marking a significant step in the government’s response to a plot that authorities say could have ended decades of democratic rule.

items.title: France reaches its "overshoot" day
items.description: This Friday, April 24, marks Earth Overshoot Day in France, a date that arrives earlier each year and signals that from now until December 31, the country is living beyond its environmental means, as explained by Elitsa Gadeva and our colleagues from France 2.

items.title: Paredes penalty ensures Superclásico seniority for Boca
items.description: While there are few frills in this current Boca team, they are tough to break down. Leandro Paredes proved up to the challenge as Xeneize secure victory in Núñez and bragging rights over River. Leer más

items.title: 'You are not alone': Pope Leo delivers message of hope to Equatorial Guinea prisoners
items.description: Pope Leo XIV told inmates at one of Equatorial Guinea’s notorious prisons that they are not alone, as he drew attention Wednesday to prison conditions, human rights abuses and injustices that campaigners have denounced for years.

items.title: UK to end project tracking potential Israeli violations
items.description: UK's Guardian newspaper reports that Foreign Office unit logging possible war crimes has shut amid funding cuts.

items.title: New mega-bridge in Lesotho to double water exports to South Africa
items.description: Every time someone in Johannesburg drinks a glass of water, there is a 60% chance it comes from neighbouring Lesotho. Now, a newly built bridge will help the tiny mountain kingdom nearly double those exports.

items.title: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi to reach Pakistan tonight for talks with U.S.: report
items.description: U.S. delegation would likely arrive in Islamabad on April 25, once the Iranian delegation works out the framework of talks with the U.S. technical team

items.title: In pictures: North India braces for heatwaves as temperatures cross 40C
items.description: India's weather department has issued heatwave warnings in parts of northern India.

items.title: Benin confirms Wadagni's presidential vote win
items.description: Benin's finance minister Romuald Wadagni was officially confirmed to have won the west African country's presidential election, according to final results on Thursday.

items.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
items.description: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

items.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
items.description: While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time — counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

items.description: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' leave camp in Syria months after failed attempt

items.title: Norway to ban social media for under-16s

items.description: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

items.title: Street art festival transforms Morocco's capital into open-air gallery

items.title: Pope Leo departs Equatorial Guinea after wrapping up 11-day trip to Africa
items.description: Pope Leo XIV on Thursday held an open-air mass in Equatorial Guinea in front of tens of thousands of followers, wrapping up his first major international tour that began with harsh criticism of his stance on Iran from US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Spain responds to reported US plans to punish NATO allies
items.description: An internal Pentagon email reportedly proposes a series of options to punish NATO allies for failing to provide support in the Iran war. Suspending Spain from the alliance was just one possibility it considered.

items.title: Russian troops, warships in India soon? Why their new military pact matters
items.description: In a significant pact, India and Russia agree to the deployment of soldiers and warships on each other's soil during peace and wartime.

items.title: Pirate gangs prowl Bangladesh's Sundarbans forest
items.description: Those who have crossed their path fear them more than the tigers in Bangladesh's Sundarbans mangrove forest. Once thought largely subdued after mass surrenders a decade ago, the gangs have returned to the forest. Their resurgence – fuelled largely by poverty and debt – has again made the forest's labyrinth of rivers feared.

items.description: Sahrawi refugees go about daily life in the Aousserd camp near Tindouf in southern Algeria as residents say decades in exile have not weakened their demand for independence in Western Sahara: "Our demands are simple -- independence, and nothing else."

items.title: Fleeing war in Sudan, refugees flock to Libya's Kufra province
items.description: For the past three years, war has been raging in Sudan betwe en the regular army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, leading to more than 11 million Sudanese being displaced. After Chad, Libya is the largest host country of Sudanese refugees. In the southeastern Kufra province, 250 kilometres from the Sudanese border, between 40 000 and 60 000 Sudanese refugees live either in encampments or towns. FRANCE 24's Lilia Blaise and Hamdi Tlili report.

items.title: UN peacekeeper dies of wounds in Lebanon
items.description: The UN mission in Lebanon said Friday that an Indonesian peacekeeper has died from wounds sustained in a March 29 attack on his base. France24 correspondent in Beirut, Renée Davis, reports the latest.

items.title: Sanchez brushes off reported Pentagon email mulling suspending Spain's NATO membership
items.description: Spain's centre-left Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday declined to comment on a reported Pentagon email written by top policy adviser Elbridge Colby raising the possibility of suspending Madrid from the NATO military alliance. The note reportedly also suggested reconsidering US support for the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands.

items.title: 'I wasn't in politics before and I'm not going to be after': Macron to quit politics in 2027
items.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday that he will quit politics in 2027 as the final year in his second term approaches. He added that at this late stage in office, the "hardest thing" was to strike a balance between defending his record and acknowledging what "didn't work out".

He also declared that humanitarian groups like UNICEF are facing an "existential funding crisis", regretting that "the world bailed out the banks" but "no one is bailing out the children".

items.description: Delaying the closure of South Africa’s coal-fired power plants could lead to up to 32,000 preventable deaths between 2026 and 2050, climate rights groups warned on Wednesday.

items.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
items.description: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Milei’s auction of Argentine railroads risks sidelining US-backed bidder
items.description: The model Milei's administration has chosen for the auctioning off of Argentina’s decaying freight railways has drawn criticism from industry experts and risks sidelining one of the most prominent bidders. Leer más

items.title: West Asia conflict could cause 120 bcm of LNG supply loss from 2026-2030, says International Energy Agency
items.description: LNG supply growth came to a halt in March after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which effectively reduced ‌the combined LNG production of Qatar and ​the United Arab Emirates by around 10 bcm for the month

items.title: Norway and Türkiye follow Australia with teen social media bans
items.description: Several European nations seek to rein in children's use of social media after Australia introduced its ban last December.

items.title: ICC says Philippines' Duterte can stand trial over war on drugs
items.description: The ICC has said there is enough evidence linking former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte to crimes against humanity over his role in orchestrating a deadly anti-drugs crackdown.

items.title: Crossing borders: The EU powers your mobility (part 1)
items.description: In this two-part show, Armen Georgian takes viewers on a journey across the European Union, exploring mobility and transport projects funded by the EU’s cohesion policy. In the 2021-2027 budget, the EU has been using its cohesion funds to invest €63.2 billion in transport schemes. From France to Austria, via Poland and Hungary, Armen takes a whistle-stop tour using seven different means of transport: train, plane, metro, tram, bicycle, scooter, and car.

items.title: Argentina's Rafael Grossi pitches ‘enlightened pragmatism’ for fractured UN
items.description: IAEA chief warns of growing distrust in the United Nations while outlining a more “pragmatic” approach to global crises; UN is not going in the right direction, he warns, offering “impartial” leadership and a front-footed approach to global crises. Leer más

items.title: Hundreds of wildfires burn across Florida and Georgia
items.description: Officials in both states cite dry conditions, high winds and drought as key factors making the fires difficult to control.

items.title: Syrian authorities arrest main suspect in 2013 Tadamon massacre
items.description: Ex-intelligence officer Amjad Youssef was seen shooting blindfolded civilians in a leaked video.

items.title: News24 | Soldier busted for betting on US capture of Venezuela’s Nicolas Maduro, making $400 000
items.description: A US Army soldier involved in the capture of Nicolas Maduro has been charged with making $400 000 ‌by betting on the removal of the ousted Venezuelan leader, the Justice Department said.

items.title: Ukraine’s Chernobyl ‘liquidators’ return 40 years after disaster
items.description: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chernobyl disaster in April 2026 as former clean-up workers returned to the site.

items.title: News24 | Iran slams US ‘blatant violation of the ceasefire’, vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
items.description: Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz - so long as a US naval blockade remained in place - was held into Thursday despite a ceasefire extension.

items.title: Indian seafarer among crew of ship held by Iran in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Of two vessels in Iranian custody, one Indian is among the 21 crew onboard Epaminondas

items.title: Milei moves to eliminate Argentina's primaries in election reform push
items.description: President announces electoral reform: elimination of PASO primaries, changes in campaign funding and introduction of ‘Ficha Limpia’ rule. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Pentagon mum on sudden departure of US Navy Secretary John Phelan, amid leadership purge
items.description: US Secretary of the Navy John Phelan will leave office “immediately”, the Pentagon announced, without providing an explanation for the sudden exit.

items.title: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
items.description: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days.

items.title: Luis Puenzo, director of Argentina’s first Oscar-winning feature, dies at 80
items.description: Luis Puenzo, director who won Argentina’s first Oscar, has died aged 80; 'La historia oficial,' which tackled kidnapping of infants during dictatorship era, won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film in 1986. Leer más

items.title: Israel retaliates against Hezbollah missile launch after ceasefire extension
items.description: Hezbollah said it had launched rockets toward Israel in retaliation for an Israeli strike on the Lebanese village of Yater

items.title: Daniel Noboa’s ‘new Ecuador’ has some fearing loss of civil freedoms
items.description: Ecuador's Daniel Noboa appears to be consolidating power in ways that are causing growing unease in civil society. One week it’s political parties, the next it’s the courts. Now it’s the media. Leer más

items.title: Bangladesh Cricket Board withdraws Mustafizur's NOC for PSL
items.description: "The Bangladesh Cricket Board wishes to inform that, following the conclusion of the 3rd ODI against New Zealand, the team's medical staff has reviewed the condition of national team pace bowler Mr. Mustafizur Rahman," says the BCB

items.title: YPF investors say they’ll arbitrate $16-billion Argentina case
items.description: Former YPF investors who saw US$16.1-billion US judgment against Argentina thrown out on appeal last month tell trial judge that they intend to pursue international treaty arbitration and further appeals. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Iran’s new supreme leader seriously wounded, delegates power to Revolutionary Guards
items.description: Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, was seriously wounded in the US-Israeli airstrike that killed his father and predecessor, Ali Khamenei, but is mentally sharp, the New York Times reported on Thursday.

items.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
items.description: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

items.title: How long can Iran survive the US’s Hormuz blockade?
items.description: And just how long can the US hold out?

items.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
items.description: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

items.title: Syria arrests main suspect in civil war Tadamon massacre
items.description: Syrian authorities have arrested Amjad Yousef, the main suspect in the 2013 Tadamon massacre in Damascus.

items.title: Milei ends visit to Israel with song at Independence Day ceremony
items.description: Argentina's President watched Independence Day ceremony from the front row, alongside Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, before taking to the stage for a speech and a song. Leer más

items.title: Cuba's ambassador to Mexico says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
items.description: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, Cuba's ambassador to Mexico blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

items.title: Man, 72, charged with decade-old murder of football great's brother
items.description: Victoria Police charge a Colac man with murder as part of its investigation into the 2012 suspicious disappearance of St Kilda football champion Barry Breen's brother, Brendan Breen.

items.title: Sheng Siong says it will keep prices affordable amid Iran war cost pressures
items.description: The supermarket also said it does not foresee any major disruptions that would materially affect the availability of essential products at its stores.

items.title: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
items.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

items.title: More than 50% of children in Argentina are poor, says UCA report
items.description: According to a report by the Observatory of Social Debt at the Catholic University of Argentina (UCA), poverty rate among children stood at 53.6% at the end of 2025. Leer más

items.title: Zambian government takes possession of ex-president Lungu's remains
items.description: Lusaka has been in a months-long dispute with Lungu's family over where the former president should be buried. Lungu died in South Africa last year.

items.title: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
items.description: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

items.title: Tornado sweeps through Oklahoma City
items.description: On Thursday, a tornado of rare intensity swept through the city of #Enid, Oklahoma, leaving behind a scene of devastation. The disaster left at least 10 people injured. Footage of the event has gone viral on social media.

items.title: Charter sheds more broadband customers than expected in first quarter
News24 | Netanyahu reveals prostate cancer treatment after delaying report during war
items.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu belatedly released his annual medical report on Friday, saying doctors had successfully removed a small “early-stage malignant tumour” from his prostate.

items.title: News24 | Israel, Lebanon extend ceasefire, Iran-US talks at a standstill as ‘clock is ticking’
items.description: Israel and Lebanon extended a shaky ceasefire by three weeks, US President Donald Trump said, as the United States remained at a standstill in negotiations with Iran to end the Middle East war.

items.title: In Ivory Coast, Abidjan’s young people wage war on trash
items.description: In a working-class neighbourhood of Abidjan, a small group of young volunteers armed with shovels and rakes is scrubbing rubbish-clogged drains. Their message: “Our streets are not rubbish bins.”

items.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
items.description: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

items.title: Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup
items.description: An envoy to United States President Donald Trump world football's governing body FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at the upcoming World Cup, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

items.title: Watch: Above 92% turnout in Bengal, 85% in Tamil Nadu amid poll violence | Above the Fold | 23.04.2026
items.description: In today’s episode, we look at the key election trends in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, tracking major shifts ahead of polling. We also cover the political row in the United States over birthright citizenship, rising tensions in West Asia around the Strait of Hormuz, and a significant change in West Bengal’s campaign landscape, and more
